notes: NI Symphony Series String Ensemble — large orchestral string section recorded at Teldex Studio Berlin. Uses a dual-dynamics approach: CC 1 (Modulation) cross-fades the p/mp/mf/f dynamic layers; CC 11 (Expression) rides the overall level. For realistic phrasing, automate both simultaneously — CC 1 shapes the tonal character, CC 11 the volume envelope. Articulations are key-switched (default range: C0–B0). Legato transition quality depends on playing with a sustained overlap — hold the first note while pressing the next. Round-robin is active; repeated notes will naturally vary.

Expression.description: Expression — rides the output level envelope. Use for phrasing crescendos and diminuendos. Combine with CC 1 for natural orchestral swells.

Modulation.description: Dynamics cross-fade — morphs between soft (p) and loud (ff) dynamic layers. This controls tone colour as well as level. The most important CC for orchestral realism.

Sustain.description: Sustain pedal — holds legato mode open on some articulations

Quick Control 1.description: Ensemble Size — scales the section between chamber (few players) and full orchestral string body

Quick Control 2.description: Close Mic Level — spot/close microphone; more bite and bow noise

Quick Control 3.description: Tree Mic Level — main stereo tree; natural blend of the full section

Quick Control 4.description: Hall / Outrigger Mic Level — wide hall ambience; room depth and spaciousness

Quick Control 5.description: Attack Shape — bow-on-string attack speed; low = soft sul tasto feel, high = aggressive spiccato entry

Quick Control 6.description: Release Time — string decay tail after note release

Quick Control 7.description: Vibrato Depth — intensity of natural player vibrato (0 = straight tone)

Quick Control 8.description: Humanise — random detuning and timing variation between desk pairs

Brightness.description: Tone brightness — high-shelf filter across the section

Stereo Width.description: Stereo image width of the full string section
